title: Podman stats with no containers is the same as podman stats --all

comments.body: One nit I'm pretty sure is going to trip gofmt, otherwise LGTM
@mheon apparently stats should run with no containers, given the test that is failing. Is it worth changing the tests, or is this not really what we want functionality wise?

> ... apparently stats should run with no containers, given the test that is failing.

Is there a `stats`-related failure?  It may just be flakes.  For example, [the smoketested][1] error:

```
setsebool:  SELinux is disabled.
neverallow check failed at /var/lib/selinux/targeted/tmp/modules/100/base/cil:9191
  (neverallow base_typeattr_7 unlabeled_t (file (entrypoint)))
    <root>
    allow at /var/lib/selinux/targeted/tmp/modules/200/container/cil:1554
      (allow spc_t unlabeled_t (file (entrypoint)))
    <root>
    allow at /var/lib/selinux/targeted/tmp/modules/100/sandboxX/cil:866
      (allow sandbox_x_domain exec_type (file (entrypoint)))
    <root>
    allow at /var/lib/selinux/targeted/tmp/modules/100/virt/cil:1677
      (allow virtd_lxc_t exec_type (file (entrypoint)))
    <root>
    allow at /var/lib/selinux/targeted/tmp/modules/100/virt/cil:2068
      (allow svirt_sandbox_domain exec_type (file (entrypoint)))

Failed to generate binary
/usr/sbin/semodule:  Failed!
```

[1]: https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/80e11b8e583c62cb6dd2bb51925c2b516b7f68ae.1.1530306507759663393/output.log

Travis is going to fail, we broke it just prior to release today (one of the tests times out on travis nodes - oops. Got to increase the timeout there). The others look like there might be a legitimate problem. Let me test this.

Alright, Docker does allow stats with no container IDs, and seems to interpret it as `--all`... So we may be doing the right thing.

Still, we do have a bug here - we're not printing the header if there are no containers. Docker does do that.
@wking that is a bad libsemange package that we have worked around until it gets updated in fedora.  SHould be soon.  For now you should ignore.
@haircommander Ok lets have podman stats <NOARGS> imply --all

:pushpin: Commit 8653398 has been approved by `rhatdan`
